metadata_information.title: The return of "patrimonial capitalism" : review of Thomas Piketty's capital in the 21st century
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: The return of "patrimonial capitalism" : review of Thomas Piketty's capital in the 21st century

document_description.abstract: The paper provides a detailed review of Thomas Piketty's book "Capital in the 21st century." It focuses on the new contributions of the book, and in particular on its unified treatment of economic growth, functional income distribution, and concentration of personal income. It concludes that Piketty's reinvigoration of classical and empircally-drven approach is likely to have a profound impact on economics.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
